questions.question: Where had Paul and his companions suffered before coming to Thessalonica?
questions.feedback: Paul mentions having previously suffered and been mistreated at Philippi.

questions.question: Paul compared his care for the Thessalonians to what?
options: A nursing mother caring for her children
questions.correct: A nursing mother caring for her children
questions.feedback: Paul said he was gentle among them, like a nursing mother caring for her own children.

questions.question: Paul says he worked night and day so as not to be a burden to the Thessalonians.
questions.feedback: Paul reminded them that he labored night and day to not burden any of them while preaching.

questions.question: Paul says he was not seeking glory from _____ but could have been a burden as apostles of Christ.
questions.feedback: Paul did not seek glory from people, even though as apostles they could have asserted authority.

questions.question: Paul called the Thessalonians his glory and _____.
questions.feedback: Paul says the Thessalonians are his glory and joy.
